symptoms.symptom_name: Severe diffuse non-anatomical body pain (neck/shoulders/back) after a psychological trigger

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute autonomic/panic crisis with somatic pain flare (trauma-related)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse, out-of-proportion, stress-flaring pain fits central sensitisation / autonomic arousal.
Severe body/pelvic pain in an IPV context must have concealed injury excluded in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute breathlessness with racing/pounding heart in fear-linked waves

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute autonomic/panic crisis with somatic pain flare (trauma-related)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Episodic, fear-triggered dyspnoea with palpitations is characteristic of panic/autonomic surge.
Breathlessness with tachycardia cannot be attributed to panic remotely without ECG/troponin.
Acute dyspnoea/tachycardia warrants PE consideration; not excludable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypervigilance, exaggerated startle, window-checking, on-edge state

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute autonomic/panic crisis with somatic pain flare, sequela of complex PTSD
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperarousal cluster is core to trauma-related presentations.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ongoing intimate partner violence with active safety concern (packed bag, financial entrapment)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute autonomic/panic crisis with somatic pain flare, sequela of complex PTSD in IPV context
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The IPV context is the causative driver of the trauma sequela and the primary safety concern.
IPV raises the pre-test probability of concealed physical injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: Negative screen for suicidal ideation / self-harm

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative, sensitively-obtained SI screen lowers immediate self-harm risk but must be documented and revisited.

symptoms.symptom_name: No acute physical assault or new injury reported today (patient self-report)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Self-report of no assault lowers but does not exclude concealed injury; not sufficient for remote exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: No crushing central chest pressure, no radiation to arm/jaw (patient report)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Atypical/non-classic character lowers ACS probability but does not exclude it without ECG/troponin.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse chest-wall and musculoskeletal tenderness reproducible on light palpation, out of proportion, no bruising/peritonism

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute autonomic/panic crisis with somatic pain flare (central sensitisation)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reproducible non-anatomical tenderness supports a central pain syndrome — a sealed in-person finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain.
Absence of focal bruising/peritonism on examination lowers injury probability — but this is exactly the exam telehealth cannot do.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vital signs: BP 142/90, HR 110, RR 24, SpO2 98%, Temp 36.6

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute autonomic/panic crisis with somatic pain flare
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachycardia, tachypnoea and mild hypertension with normal SpO2 fit an autonomic surge — but these are clinician-measured values sealed from the AI Doctor, who must infer arousal from the patient's subjective report.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG / troponin to exclude acute coronary syndrome

investigation_details.expected_result: Not performed in the source; required in person to exclude ACS
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: ACS cannot be excluded; attributing symptoms to panic without it risks a missed infarct.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ECG/troponin is the objective exclusion test — never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute autonomic/panic crisis, sequela of complex PTSD in IPV context
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these point to a trauma-driven autonomic crisis — but do not, by themselves, exclude the organic mimics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person clinical examination (chest wall, abdomen, injury survey, vitals)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes occult trauma/internal injury and characterises the pain — the exam telehealth cannot perform.
Objective exclusion of acute coronary syndrome for the breathlessness/tachycardia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Structured IPV safety/danger assessment (e.g. Danger Assessment) at next in-person contact
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies imminent-harm risk and guides safety planning; must be done sensitively and confidentially.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine broad blood panel / extensive imaging ordered reflexively at first contact
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Do not over-investigate remotely or reflexively; targeted in-person assessment is the correct next step, not a scattergun workup.
